bids.reasoning: At t=0.0 there is no need to concede materially, so I am targeting a near-maximum utility bid. The opponent’s opening offer gives me low utility on several important issues, but they selected issueD:valueB and issueE:valueA, which are acceptable to me; I can keep issueD:valueB at no cost while holding firm on my other high-value issues.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, and the opponent has simply repeated the same low-utility offer to me rather than showing meaningful concession. I am therefore targeting a very high-utility bid while avoiding an exact repeat of my last offer, making only a small concession on issueA where valueB is still nearly as good for me as valueA.

At t=0.04 it is still very early, and the opponent has repeated the same low-utility offer for me rather than conceding, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making only a small tradeoff. I keep firm on the high-importance issues where my preferred values matter most, while changing a lower-weight issue from my prior bid to avoid simply repeating myself.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid around 0.98 because it is still very early in the negotiation and the opponent has repeated the same low-utility offer to me without conceding. Their bids consistently include issueD:valueB and issueE:valueA, which are good for me, so I keep those while making only a small concession on issueA and holding firm on my high-value positions for issueB and issueC.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still early at t=0.08 and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeating the same low-utility-to-me offer. At t=0.10 it is still early, so I targeted a high-utility bid while making a modest additional concession to avoid stalemate. The opponent has mostly held firm but recently moved issueC from valueC to valueB, so I am reciprocating on lower-cost dimensions while holding my more important/high-cost preferences.

At t=0.12 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.90 rather than moving close to the opponent’s latest offer. I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.92 because it is still early at t=0.14, but the opponent has begun conceding toward higher-utility options for me on issueC and most recently issueA. At t=0.16 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.90 rather than making large concessions. The opponent has started conceding on issueB and issueC while consistently keeping issueD=valueB and issueE=valueA, so I keep strong positions on the high-weight issues while conceding issueF and slightly softening issueA compared with my maximum bid.

I am targeting a strong but more accommodating utility of about 0.844, since it is still early at t=0.18 and there is no need to make deep concessions yet.
